name: Ruta da Liberdade - Barbeira River Trail
name_original: Barbeira desde la Playa fluvial de Verdugo — Ruta da Liberdade (A Lama)
description: Hike the moderate 10.3-mile Ruta da Liberdade in A Lama, exploring oak forests, riverbanks, and mountain pastures with wild horses.
region_intro: Family friendly hiking trails in Serra Do Cando traverse a diverse landscape of lush forests, extensive oak groves, and open scrubland. The region is characterized by its mountainous terrain, part of the Galician Dorsal Range, with elevations reaching up to 1000 meters. Rivers like the Verdugo and Seixo contribute to scenic river valleys and waterfalls, such as Cascada de Cavadosa. This protected environment, a Natura 2000 reserve, offers a variety of paths suitable for families. Best family friendly hiking trails in Serra Do Cando The most popular family friendly hiking route is Cascada de Cavadosa from Cerdedo — circular by the río Seixo, a 10.0 miles (16.1 km) trail that takes 4 hours 50 minutes to complete. This moderate route leads to the Cascada de Cavadosa waterfall. Another top favourite among local hikers is Ruta da Liberdade - Barbeira River Trail, a moderate 10.3 miles (16.5 km) path. It offers scenic views along the Barbeira River through varied natural environments. Local hikers also love the Cerdedo — circular by the Sendero Frei Martin Sarmiento, a 6.4 miles (10.3 km) trail leading through forests and countryside, often completed in about 3 hours 17 minutes. Hiking in Serra Do Cando is defined by ancient woodlands, river valleys, and mountainous ascents. The network offers options for different ability levels, including 2 easy and 10 moderate family friendly hiking trails. The routes in Serra Do Cando are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.8 stars from more than 30 reviews. More than 150 hikers have used komoot to explore Serra Do Cando's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many family-friendly hiking trails are available in Serra Do Cando?
A: Serra Do Cando offers a good selection of family-friendly hiking trails. Our guide features 15 routes, with 2 categorized as easy and 10 as moderate, making them suitable for families with varying experience levels.

Q: Are there any easy family-friendly trails suitable for beginners?
A: Yes, there are several easy trails perfect for families and beginners. While many routes are moderate, you'll find paths that are less challenging, often following river valleys or through gentle woodlands. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31862983" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Verdugo River Beach – Pasos da Fraga Stepping Stones loop from O Verdugo&lt;/a&gt; offers a pleasant experience with minimal elevation gain.

Q: Can we find trails with waterfalls in Serra Do Cando?
A: Absolutely! The region is known for its scenic river valleys and impressive waterfalls. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1374556162/cascada-de-cavadosa-from-cerdedo-circular-by-the-rio-seixo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cascada de Cavadosa from Cerdedo — circular by the río Seixo&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate trail that leads directly to the beautiful Cascada de Cavadosa waterfall, a highlight for many families.

Q: What kind of landscapes can we expect on family hikes in Serra Do Cando?
A: Family hikes in Serra Do Cando offer a rich variety of landscapes. You'll encounter lush forests, extensive oak groves, open scrubland, and meadows. Many trails follow scenic river valleys, and you'll often see unique granite formations sculpted by nature. The western slopes, in particular, are known for their dense, vibrant vegetation.

Q: Are there any circular routes suitable for families?
A: Yes, many of the trails in Serra Do Cando are circular, which is ideal for family outings as you end up back where you started. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1374556162/cascada-de-cavadosa-from-cerdedo-circular-by-the-rio-seixo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cascada de Cavadosa from Cerdedo — circular by the río Seixo&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1374445255/cerdedo-circular-by-the-sendero-frei-martin-sarmiento" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerdedo — circular by the Sendero Frei Martin Sarmiento&lt;/a&gt;, both offering diverse scenery.

Q: What are some interesting sights or landmarks to look out for on these trails?
A: Beyond the natural beauty, Serra Do Cando's trails offer several points of interest. You might encounter traditional Galician hamlets, old mills along rivers, and unique geological formations. Notable attractions include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2337736" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Verdugo River Beach&lt;/a&gt;, the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2730483" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Three Estuaries Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, and the natural monument &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5823778" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Porta do Alén&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2518075" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monte Seixo&lt;/a&gt; also offers panoramic views.

Q: Is Serra Do Cando a good place to spot wildlife with kids?
A: Serra Do Cando is a protected environment, making it a great place for wildlife spotting. While elusive Iberian wolves are present, you're more likely to see roe deer, wild boar, and foxes. Birdwatchers might spot goshawks, sparrowhawks, and tawny owls, especially in the dense tree cover. It's a wonderful opportunity for children to connect with nature.

Q: What do other hikers say about the family-friendly trails in Serra Do Cando?
A: The komoot community highly rates the trails in Serra Do Cando, with an average score of 3.78 stars. Reviewers often praise the diverse terrain, from lush forests to river valleys, and the authentic hiking experience provided by ancient pathways. Many appreciate the well-preserved natural environment and the scenic beauty.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking with family in Serra Do Cando?
A: The best time to hike with family in Serra Do Cando is generally during spring and autumn. These seasons offer pleasant temperatures, vibrant foliage, and less extreme weather conditions. Summer can also be enjoyable, especially on shaded forest trails or near rivers, but it's wise to start early to avoid the midday heat. Winters are cooler and wetter, but can be beautiful for those prepared for the conditions.

Q: Are there any trails that are particularly good for younger children or strollers?
A: While many trails in Serra Do Cando involve varied terrain, some sections along river paths or through flatter woodlands can be suitable for younger children. For strollers, look for routes explicitly marked as accessible or paved, though these are less common on natural hiking trails. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31862983" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Verdugo River Beach – Pasos da Fraga Stepping Stones loop from O Verdugo&lt;/a&gt; might offer some more accessible sections, but always check the route details for specific terrain information.

Q: What should we consider regarding parking when planning a family hike?
A: When planning a family hike in Serra Do Cando, it's advisable to check the starting points of your chosen routes for available parking. Many trailheads near villages or popular attractions will have designated parking areas, though they might be limited. Arriving earlier in the day, especially on weekends, can help secure a spot. Always ensure you park responsibly and do not obstruct local access.

Q: Are there any routes that follow rivers or have prominent water features?
A: Yes, the region is rich in water features. The Verdugo and Seixo rivers, along with their tributaries, create beautiful river valleys. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1374420493/ruta-da-liberdade-barbeira-river-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruta da Liberdade - Barbeira River Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a great example, offering scenic views along the Barbeira River. These routes often provide refreshing stops and opportunities for children to explore the riverside environment.
tips: Sierra de Candó crossing. Continue to the right and you will go up to the Miradoiro das Tres Rías. We turn left to go down to the Barbeira River.
The O Peso bridge is one of the two bridges that cross the Barbeira River between the limits of the municipalities of Cotobade and A Lama.
Wild swimming is a pleasure, you must try it. This place, like many in Pontevedra, is well maintained and accessible to the public bathroom. You must jump on a warm summer afternoon.

faqs.question: What is the overall difficulty of the Barbeira from the Playa fluvial de Verdugo — Ruta da Liberdade (A Lama) trail?
faqs.answer: This route is generally considered moderate. While some sections along the Verdugo River are relatively easy, the full 16.5 km length and nearly 450 meters of elevation gain mean hikers should be prepared for varied terrain, including potentially muddy and uneven ground, especially where livestock have been.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this hike?
faqs.answer: The trail offers diverse terrain. You'll start along the banks of the Río Barbeira, winding through native oak forests with intensely green, mossy paths. As you ascend towards the Serra do Cando, the terrain becomes more elevated, featuring a 'camino empedrado' (cobblestone path) and open areas where wild horses graze. Expect some parts to be muddy or trodden by livestock.

faqs.question: Are there any notable landmarks or natural features along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is rich in features. You'll encounter the Sierra de Candó Crossing, offering impressive valley views, and the picturesque Bridge over the Barbeira River. The route also passes by the Verdugo River Beach, and historically significant points like the Church of Santa Minia and the Chapel of Nosa Señora de Lourdes.

faqs.question: Is the Barbeira from the Playa fluvial de Verdugo — Ruta da Liberdade (A Lama) trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally dog-friendly. However, as parts of the route pass through areas where wild horses and livestock may be present, it's advisable to keep dogs on a leash for their safety and to respect the local wildlife and farming practices.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed year-round, but spring and autumn are often ideal. During these seasons, the weather is typically milder, and the natural environment, especially the oak forests, is particularly vibrant. Summers can be warm, and some sections might be muddier after heavy rains.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead?
faqs.answer: The trail typically starts from the Playa fluvial de Verdugo (Verdugo River Beach) area in A Lama. There is usually parking available near the beach, which serves as a convenient starting point for this circular route.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or is there an entrance fee for the Sierra de Candán area?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike this trail or to access the general Sierra de Candán area where it is located. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Does this trail connect with any other hiking routes?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with other paths. Approximately 6% of this trail overlaps with sections of the official Roteiro da Escuadra.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or restaurants near the trailhead?
faqs.answer: The Playa fluvial de Verdugo area in A Lama often has some basic amenities, especially during peak seasons. For a wider selection of cafes, pubs, and restaurants, you would typically find options within the nearby town of A Lama.

faqs.question: What is the historical significance of the name 'Ruta da Liberdade'?
faqs.answer: The name 'Ruta da Liberdade' (Freedom Route) has a unique historical origin. It's said that prisoners once worked on maintaining sections of this path, and their good conduct earned them reduced sentences, hence the association with 'freedom'.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this hike?
faqs.answer: Given the trail's moderate difficulty and varied terrain, we recommend sturdy hiking boots, sufficient water, snacks, and appropriate clothing layers. A map or GPS device is also advisable, as some sections can be less defined. Trekking poles can be helpful for stability on uneven or muddy ground.
